prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Review this session and write the first version of CLAUDE.md at the training-directory root.

risks_fired.evidence: Every rule you write should be traceable to a specific moment in the session where the rule either helped or would have helped.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Intentional residual — Debrief prompts carry self-report risk by design (the audit IS the lesson per 2026-05-02-pedagogy-debrief-prompts-residual-med-risk-by-design.md). Body prose already addresses the tell ('what Claude leaves out is often the signal'). If escalated: add self-critique framing inside the fence — 'Flag rules you're uncertain about. If a rule has no clear session anchor, name the gap rather than inferring one.'

risks_fired.evidence: tell me in 3-5 lines: the rules you wrote, which session moments grounded each one, and what you chose NOT to include and why.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Intentional residual — body prose follow-up ('ask once: what did you choose NOT to write, and why?') is the designed counter. If sharpened: extend the exclusion framing inside the fence — 'Include at least one thing you considered writing but rejected — and the honest reason why.'

risks_fired.evidence: Review this session and write the first version of CLAUDE.md... Cover at minimum: the memory... the sources... the agents... and whatever else earned its place.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Intentional residual — Debrief is a send-off; preamble is low-cost here. If sharpened: prime the student that Claude will narrate structure before writing; tell them to look for the rules file itself, not the opening narration.
